title: SQL: count(*) alias in subselect
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Alice'), (2, 'Bob'), (3, NULL);
SELECT count(*) AS total_count FROM test_table;
SELECT total_count FROM (SELECT count(*) AS total_count FROM test_table) AS subquery;
SELECT name FROM (SELECT name AS x FROM test_table WHERE name IS NOT NULL) AS t;
